description: Figure 7 presents three line graphs showing the calculated evolution of deformation-induced martensite (DIM) and retained austenite (RA) volume fractions versus true strain for a Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si low-density steel processed via intercritical annealing at 750°C, 800°C, and 850°C (followed by tempering at 400°C). Based on the Olson-Cohen (O-C) kinetic model with fitting parameters α=5, β=1.28, and n=0.55, each sub-plot displays dual-axis curves: black lines (left y-axis) represent the increasing DIM fraction, while red lines (right y-axis) depict the decreasing RA fraction during deformation. The three panels correspond to samples with initial RA contents of approximately 7.4%, 2.4%, and 0.6%, respectively, reaching final DIM fractions of 4.74%, 1.16%, and 0.28% at the indicated strains. The figure quantifies the TRIP effect kinetics, demonstrating that strain-induced martensitic transformation diminishes with higher annealing temperatures due to reduced RA stability and content.
